assumptions: No monday.com account, product, workspace, board, confirmed email, eligible user role, Developer mode setting, Developer Center access, personal token, or prior API request is assumed.
A browser and a terminal or supported language runtime are available.
No signup, trial, account activation, token access, or API request was performed during research.

developer_goal: Create a monday.com trial or developer account, enable Developer mode, copy a personal V2 API token, and make the official first GraphQL API request.

first_success.outcome: The intended terminal is a token-authenticated POST to `https://api.monday.com/v2` containing one of the guide's board queries.
first_success.observable_signal: The guide demonstrates a JSON response whose `data.boards` array contains a board ID and name, plus an `account_id`; a transport response or empty data alone should not be treated as the demonstrated board result.

route_selection.surface: monday platform API first GraphQL request with a personal token
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: monday platform API first GraphQL request with a personal token
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: API playground
candidates.reason: The first-request guide lists the playground as an exploration option after authentication; its published language examples are the direct first request.

candidates.name: OAuth app token
candidates.reason: The authentication guide uses a personal V2 token for a user's own account, while app tokens add OAuth and scope configuration.

candidates.name: Platform MCP
candidates.reason: This is a separate tool surface; the API guide explicitly defines the first GraphQL request route.

required_path.action: Complete monday.com account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new monday.com account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected monday.com candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The guide demonstrates a JSON response whose `data.boards` array contains a board ID and name, plus an `account_id`; a transport response or empty data alone should not be treated as the demonstrated board result.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'Create a monday.com trial account or a developer account that is strictly for developing apps.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S4', 'S5']}

{'order': 2, 'type': 'verification', 'requirement': 'Have a confirmed and enabled user account; unconfirmed, deactivated, disabled, viewer, and student users cannot access the API.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S3']}

{'order': 3, 'type': 'permission', 'requirement': 'Use an admin or member role for direct access to a personal API token; guests require OAuth or a short-lived token instead.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2', 'S3']}

external_gates.description: The first-request guide presents trial and developer accounts without selecting one for a new developer.
Both signup destinations hide the current account and onboarding sequence in a client-rendered application.
Only eligible confirmed users can access the API, and personal-token availability depends on user type.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Making your first request to the monday platform API.

excluded.item: Board-item queries, mutations, application packaging, OAuth, webhooks, API analytics, pagination, complexity limits, production token rotation, and Platform MCP use occur after the first GraphQL respo
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

sources.title: Making your first request
evidence_supported: Primary documented developer route and first-success framing

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does monday.com show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
